steps.title: Recognize what 'success' means for this program
steps.content: The passage describes a program that breeds and reintroduces condors to the wild. For the biologists' claim of “reasonable success” to be supported, the graph data should show the wild population growing.

steps.title: Find the wild population growth in the data
steps.content: D shows the wild population increased overall from about 230 to 330 (2014–2020) and exceeded the captive population in 2020 (330 vs. 180), directly demonstrating the reintroduction program has succeeded. The other choices either inaccurately describe the trends, focus on insignificant short-term changes, or fail to demonstrate the wild population growth necessary to support a success claim. That matches choice D — D is correct.
